content: Backtesting is the process of evaluating a trading strategy using historical data to simulate how it would have performed. Proper backtesting requires accounting for transaction costs, slippage, survivorship bias, and look-ahead bias. A robust backtest uses out-of-sample data for validation and includes walk-forward analysis to verify strategy stability across different market regimes.
source: Quantitative Trading: How to Build Your Own Algorithmic Trading Business
